mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.6% in May 2013 suggests ongoing labor market recovery from 2008 crisis
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volumes signal robust consumer demand and economic expansion, which increases corporate profit expectations and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 421.1 billion in May 2013 reflects sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with RSAFS growth rate (Spearman rho < 0.08).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 1.5% while inflation remains below 2.5%, the Fed is tightening into weak demand conditions, signaling policy error that constrains equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.11% in May 2013 indicates accommodative stance
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with subdued inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.015 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals increasing manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, which supports corporate margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.67 in May 2013 shows gradual recovery from crisis lows
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive association with month-over-month INDPRO changes (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline below 800k units while unemployment remains elevated above 7%, signaling weak housing demand and constrained household formation, which reduces future consumer spending and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 853k in April 2013, UNRATE at 7.6% in May 2013
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with persistent labor slack
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates above 200k per month while unemployment declines, signaling strong labor market momentum that increases consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month average PAYEMS growth > 200k AND UNRATE declining
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135.6 million in May 2013 with ongoing recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in triggered periods do not exceed non-triggered baseline by at least 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation below 2% combined with federal funds rate near zero signals monetary accommodation that reduces real discount rates and increases equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 231.49 in April 2013, FEDFUNDS at 0.11% in May 2013
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.008 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate while retail sales growth slows below 3%, signaling weakening aggregate demand that constrains corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Quarterly GDPC1 growth < 2.0% annualized AND 3-month RSAFS growth < 3.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13746.2 billion in Q1 2013, RSAFS at 421.1 billion in May 2013
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.05) in any 2-year rolling window.
